transcript.text: On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there, good evening to you. This is TV on the radio, Thomas Vance, and welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening, with their first ever Radio 1 session, you can hear a Suffolk-based band called Trespass,
and also a repeat of a set we recorded with Money some time ago that includes their track called Another Case of Suicide.
And we've got classic cuts from Neil Young, Hawkwind, Genesis, and Rush, and also something strange from a mystery guest.
We've got new stuff by Shaking Street, Riot, Grace Slick, Peter Gabriel, and not forgetting our Friday Night Connection, the answer to the one we set for you a fortnight ago.
And this evening we start with Crocus.
[Music]
That's Neil Young and Crazy Horse from the album Live Rust, and the track was called Like a Hurricane.
A love especially for Graham Hickey, I think it's pronounced,
at [redacted address], Western Bath in Avon.
And also from Dave and Barney, who live in Ellesmere Port,
for the Gannett Special, 12.15, Nutjob, TA Stud, Amsterdam, Chastity and Othello.
And we've got a little card here from Graham McEwan,
who lives in Academy House, Ewanfield Road in Creef.
Possibly say hello to Robbie and Sam and Husey and Gregor and Jolly and Graham
and everybody at the Academy House, Morrison's Academy in Creef.
and I've got to tell Ruth that Graham loves him.

and for Phil Wilde of number 17, Morven Road in Dewsbury.

with regard to where they're going to be playing
between now and the 5th of June.

the first of four from their very very first ever session for radio one you would have recognized it

It has a female lead singer.
Her name is Fabienne Schein.
I think she comes from France.
The album's called Shaking Street.
It's just come out on CBS, produced by Sandy Perlman,
who's done Blue Oyster Cult and The Clash.
The track was our one-up box here.
26 and a half minutes before 11 of the clock.
In a fortnight's time, we're going to be doing a version of our flashback series.
This one is going to be Flashback 1969, built around the music and, well,
just the various things that happened in the musical world during the year of 1969.
If you've got any suggestions as to what you'd like in the programme, well, that's what we're here for.
Just jot them down on a postcard and send them to Flashback 69, The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London W1A4WW.
And we'll do the rest. And here now is money.
and just another case of suicide had a letter from john stanhope who lives in peckham in london

9th of May, at the Norbrecht Castle in Blackpool. Saturday, the 10th of May, the Rex Club, Driffiel
near Hull. Friday, 30th of May, North East London Poly, Livingston House, Stratford in

There's something very special about Kit Jensen's show on Monday afternoon.
The whole of the first half will feature a concert by The Police,
which was especially recorded by Radio 1 at Newcastle City Hall.
And in order that you can enjoy it to the full,
It'll be broadcast in stereo on VHF, as well as 275 and 285 meters medium wave.
And it all happens this Monday, just after 4.30.
So make sure you join Andy Stewart and Sting as they police the Radio 1 airwaves
in the first half of Kid Jensen's show this Monday at 4.30
on 275, 285 meters medium wave and in stereo on VHF.

Three pieces of music with a theme.
See if you can figure it out.
And that's it.
Our Friday night connection for tonight.
Put together by Alan Patton, who lives up there in Glasgow.
Three bits of music that have a very definite theme.
Not too difficult either.
If you think you know what the theme is,
then just jot it down on a postcard.
Any form of communication will do.
Send it along to The Friday Night Connection, The Friday Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
But don't forget, please, to put together your own Friday Night Connection as well and jot it on that communication.
It's a £10 record token. Good luck.
It's 11.20. Now back to Trespass, who incidentally are Mark Sutcliffe and Paul Sutcliffe.
Mark plays the lead guitar and does the backing vocals.
Paul does the drums.

and here now is our final track from our ace session by Trespass.

It's Live It Up.
And that's a track from a new compilation.
The compilation album's just out on MCA.
